summary: Reverse shell and Kubernetes credential exfiltration to hardcoded C2
details: index.js is an IIFE that executes on require()/import and opens a reverse shell by spawning /bin/bash wired to a TCP socket at hardcoded IP 103.27.109.184:8895, giving the remote party interactive shell control of the installer's host. The same module reads /home/ubuntu/.kube/config, parses the bearer token and API server, collects host recon (hostname, hostname -I, ip route, iptables, internal Kubernetes API probes) and POSTs the JSON blob to 103.27.109.184:8890. It also downloads kubectl from dl.k8s.io to /tmp/kubectl, chmods it executable, and runs it against the harvested kubeconfig to interact with the victim's Kubernetes cluster. The package name suggests an n8n custom node integration, but the shipped code is a targeted attack against installer hosts and their Kubernetes clusters.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
